items.title: [负面] Fun idea for the world and mechanics, but the laby...
items.summary: Fun idea for the world and mechanics, but the labyrinth of gacha mini-tasks, resource juggling and lack of meaningful decisions in combat kept me thinking that the game is playing me instead of me playing the game. There's no sense of progress, because there's no challenge in the battles. I wish the developers spent more time on polishing the core mechanics of the game instead of bloating it with underwhelming and unnecessary extra features. Oh, and not being able to scroll the dialogue box and the amount of clicking that's required to progress through card acquisition is too much. For me it was interesting enough to try but in the end doesn't have enough meat to make it more than a waste of time.

items.title: [负面] fun if you like mobile games. It wants your money ...
items.summary: fun if you like mobile games. It wants your money really bad. I kind of don't like that. Enemies are incredibly generic. The story is too. You're seeing the same backgrounds over and over again and the AI generated translations makes the ongoing barrage of texts worth skipping altogether. Its disappointing bc I would actually invest time and money into something like this if there was potential for it to improve visually and conceptually, but I seriously doubt my money will go toward anything but a predatory market strategy at this point. Mtg lets you earn the secondary currency slowly over time by competing in drafts. It keeps the pool of players consistent and actually adds an incentive to spend money for more of the secondary contrary to what you'd originally think. The devs in this game could do well to learn from other established digital card game's bottom lines as it seriously kills the genuinely fun parts about this game.
